what_it_does: A session-reconstruction 'transfer pack' for the DNA/design-render lane of the counterpart/design fabric, meant to be read after compaction so a resumed instance can pick up state, priorities, recent decisions, anti-patterns, and coordination context.
observations: Session id 93d518a6-9510-4706-8250-0518adc96c95 is given explicitly because no COMPANY_SESSION_ID is injected.
The file is opinion-laden and repeatedly warns the reader to trust the factual repo record (DNA-REPO-FACTS.md) over its own appraisals.
It defines a 'bar' where the unit of done is a closed loop verified by Tim actually using the surface, not a rendered screenshot.
It lists a build priority order: tokenise recipes, artefact organism family, interaction states, blueprint systems, N-panel/workflow archetypes, responsive resolver.
It documents a 'keystone' two-panel decision render (merge-sa authorize) and its current not-yet-Tim-verified status.
It enumerates a FACE-1 breadth queue: session-card, decision-cards, channel-view, board-view, timeline-lanes, transcript-viz, nav, L5 update, address-view, embedded-CLI, supervisor brain.
It records Tim's correction that the real gap is design dimensions (size, space, colour, shadow, position, density of generated symbols), not photos vs line-art.
It references a 24-page design-language extraction saved to docs/command/DESIGN-LANGUAGE-EXTRACT.md and a full ~160-page extraction plan.
It notes a fresh-budget handoff queue for the next DNA instance: slot_types/design-language, timeline-lanes + address-view, visual-dna re-bake fields.
It includes phone-reachable URLs and a warning that :5173/:8770 are localhost-only.
It ends by stating there are no code symbols in the file.
standouts: Tim's reframe: synthetic must be indistinguishable from real; the failure axis is design dimensions, not asset type.
The 'bar' is Tim-use verified; renders and fabric-by-use are explicitly not enough.
The keystone is currently worse/empty after a system-level correction and must be rebuilt from the design-language synthesis, not band-aided.
A mapped FACE-1 render queue with per-surface adapter pattern (DNA.faceRecord.* → renderArchetype).
The 'default-to-wrong' / 'don't self-grade' discipline is treated as a recurring anti-pattern, not a one-time fix.
conventions: Uses inline status emojis (✅ ⚠️ ⏳ ★), ISO timestamps, thread IDs (g-...), commit hashes, and channel handles (ch-...).
Heavy use of explicit prohibitions: 'do NOT call this done', 'do NOT self-certify', 'do NOT build ahead of align'.
Self-correcting log style: earlier reads are crossed out and replaced with later corrections.
References an external memory index and recall tools rather than embedding full history.
Mixes roadmap, working notes, coordination messages, and lessons in one document.
concerns: The document contains rapidly reversed verdicts (e.g. photo-composite was built, then labeled a wrong-axis detour and abandoned), which may leave a reader unsure of current ground truth.
It depends heavily on external context (channel threads, uncommitted files, live routes) that may be stale after compaction.
Many acronyms and shorthand (POPCORN-B, depth-5, strand #3, the 8+2 fields) are used without in-file definition.
The repeated 'do not over-claim' warnings suggest a history of over-claiming that the document itself is trying to prevent.
It flags a contradiction between Tim's prior muted-gold decision and the extracted source evidence for bolder gold, leaving it unresolved.
notes: A dense, self-correcting handoff log that maps what the DNA lane just built, where it is blocked on Tim verification, and what the next instance should do next.
questions: What is 'depth-5' and why is it unknown?
What are the exact current shapes of /api/events, /api/decisions, and /api/timeline?
What is the current uncommitted file state at the moment of reading?
What do POPCORN-B, strand #3, and the 8+2 visual-dna fields specifically mean?
Is the phone URL http://100.81.137.106:8099/pieces/decision-card/real-merge-sa-authorize.html still reachable after compaction?
What is the precise difference between 'fabric-complete' and 'Tim-closed' in practice?
How is the design-language extraction schema applied to tokens/engine, and has that render validation happened?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be read first after compaction to reconstruct the session and resume; the content does that but is overloaded with coordination notes, anti-bias directives, and a fresh-budget handoff, so it reads as a working log rather than a clean brief.
apparent_themes: Don't self-grade; Tim's eye is the terminator.
Fix recurring problems at the system/token level, not per-card.
Build one surface at a time and verify before moving on.
Compositional generative design language over photographic compositing.
Closed-loop operator use as the definition of done.
intention_signals: The file is trying to stop the DNA lane from declaring victory on rendered screenshots and to redirect effort toward a systematic, source-extracted, generative design language that produces source-density faces, while handing off the heavy remaining strands to a fresh budget.
novelty: An unusually self-aware handoff document that embeds anti-bias directives, names its own prior over-claims, and defines completion as a human closed-loop use test rather than a build artifact.
contribution: A session-state artifact that preserves the current queue, explicit anti-patterns, decision boundaries, and coordination context for a resumed DNA lane; it contributes the mapped FACE-1 render queue and the 'verify by Tim-use' framing as guardrails.
summary_for_embedding: A post-compaction transfer pack for the DNA/design-render lane that reconstructs session state, records Tim's design-dimension reframe, maps the FACE-1 surface queue, and hands off the next heavy strands to a fresh budget.
intention_for_embedding: To orient a resumed DNA instance after compaction so it does not over-claim completion and continues the systematic design-language extraction and FACE-1 surface wiring.
